title: ‘Senior assassin’ game in Stoneham leads police to briefly detain high schooler
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Local News The teenager is heard on the body camera footage explaining he was holding a "senior assassin water gun" and apologizing to the officer.
Body camera footage showing police approach a teenager who was holding what was later determined to be a water gun.
Stoneham police Stoneham police are warning high school students who are playing the game “senior assassin” after a concerned resident called 911 reporting a man with a gun outside his home.

title: The Bruins have a clear offseason goal. How they achieve it is the real question.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins "You look at the elite teams in the league, we're not there.
” Cam Neely, Don Sweeney, and the Bruins' top brass spoke with the media on Wednesday.

title: Lowell nonprofits provide essentials, housing after fire ravages 6 houses, displaces 40 residents
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Local News Fire officials explained that the flames spread quickly due to high winds, dry conditions, and close proximity between the homes.
The Lowell community is coming together to help victims of a devastating fire Tuesday.
Ken McGagh/The Boston Globe Local nonprofits are supporting the Lowell community after a devastating fire destroyed six homes and displaced 40 residents Tuesday.

title: Video shows wrong-way driver just minutes before fatally striking state trooper
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Local News A garbage truck driver sees the wrong-way driver heading south in the northbound lanes in the Lynnfield tunnel, blares his horn and calls 911.

Boston Police Department officers line Albany Street and salute as the motorcade carrying fallen State Police Trooper Kevin Trainor passes by en route to the Medical Examiner’s office in Boston, Massachusetts May 6, 2026.
A wrong-way driver and State Police trooper Kevin Trainor were killed when their vehicles collided head-on.

title: Jaylen Brown fires back at Stephen A. Smith, tells him to ‘be quiet’ and ‘retire’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Celtics ​“He needs to be quiet … unless he wants to be traded.
" Jaylen Brown has had enough of Stephen A.
Smith.

title: Sheriff’s lieutenant’s lost dog found after extensive search; family ‘elated’
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Local News The family enlisted the help of police-grade drone technology as they searched for the missing Catahoula leopard dog.
Cash, the 3-year-old Catahoula leopard dog, was found after being missing for nearly a week.
Tanya Cubelli A lost dog belonging to a Haverhill sheriff’s lieutenant was found Thursday, nearly a week after he went missing.

title: A Dr. Seuss photo booth is coming to Boston just in time for graduates
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Books Dr.
Seuss Enterprises is adding a “highly visual” photo booth that paints homage to Seuss's picture-book "Oh, the Places You'll Go!
" the Boston Calendar announced.

title: Roman Anthony lands on injured list with hand sprain, Red Sox expect quick return
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Red Sox "For me, it was frustrating, and I started to feel good in the box.
Started to really settle in.
" Roman Anthony could return on May 15.

title: Caleb Lomu’s wife predicted his path to the Patriots, then his uncle flew him there
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: New England Patriots Lomu's uncle, an American Airlines pilot, flew the Patriots first rounder's red-eye flight to Boston Thursday morning.
Caleb Lomu at the NFL Scouting Combine.

title: 14-year-old boy faces gun charges after girl shot in Dorchester
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Crime The teen girl shot on Milton Avenue was transported to a local hospital with non-life threatening injuries, police said.
A 14-year-old boy is facing gun charges, including possession of a large capacity feeding device, after a teen girl was shot in Dorchester Wednesday night, police said.
Police responded to reports of a person shot in the area of Milton Avenue around 8 p.
